---
title: "SuperScout: Automating location intelligence for the film and screen industry"
description: "London-based software company is enabling metadata renrichment and optimised workflows for location professionals and film offices through the application of vision enabled AI" 
categories:
  - Responsible AI
  - Investment
  - Sector adaptation
author: David Johnston
citation:
  publisher: CoSTAR Foresight Lab
  container-title: "AI in the Screen Sector: Casebook"
date: 18/03/2026
toc: false
# image:
# image-alt:
---

## Background
[SuperScout]() is a London based software company building a location platform for the film and screen industry. Founded by Gabriel Isserlis, the company was born from a simple idea: create a platform to allow screen professionals to manage their assets and locations outside of Dropbox folders or spreadsheets.

The platform designed from the ground up for location professionals. It looks and feels like Airbnb, says Isserlis — locations on the left and maps on the right. It is a fully private database for each subscriber of the platform but with the ability to share with clients or agencies. After initially targeting individual location scouts, the company has found strong traction with film offices. SuperScout now has customers across the UK, USA and Europe, with English Heritage among its most prominent clients. The platform is web-based and has an iOS app; it allows users to upload photos, location data and metadata to each location. This can be done in an offline mode whilst travelling, and then as a bulk upload when back on WiFi or mobile data.


:::{.column-body}
![](images/caira_with_woman_in_bg.png){fig-alt="A photograph of a woman in a white t-shirt holding Camera Intelligence's 'Caira' product with a wide-angle lens. She is looking at the back of the camera as if focusing on the photo or videography."}
:::

::: figure-caption
A woman holds Camera Intelligence’s 2nd generation Caira camera, copyright Camera Intelligence.
:::

## Application of AI 
Every photo uploaded to SuperScout is automatically keyworded by an image recognition model. For users this is invisible, if they upload a picture of a kitchen, for example, the photos will be tagged with ‘kitchen’, ‘counter’, ‘utensils, ‘sink’ etc… Whilst there is no manual tagging step, the tags can be edited in retrospect.

For organisations managing large databases, the impact is substantial, one film office reported that uploaded a single location can take up 40 minutes with previous workflows – with SuperScout it takes less than 5, fully populated with metadata, and can be done on the road.

Getting this to work robustly was paramount for SuperScout, the metadata has to be valid and useful for busy location professionals. The team initially used the OpenAI API to access the vision-enabled models, but through testing identified challenges with certain models including cultural bias -  an example being models misidentifying non-western architecture as variations on western architecture rather than specific entities, an Indian palace may be labelled as an ‘orange medieval castle’ for example. The team moved to an open weights model via the platform HuggingFace. The workflow itself, which simultaneously extracts keywords, generates search embeddings and produces other structure metadata for the platform, took weeks of testing to refine.



::: {.column-body}
::: {.pullquote-container}
::: {.grid .gap-6 .pb-3 .pt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-9}
::: {.pullquote}
“We’re looking to make it easier for people to express themselves, tell their stories. We are very conscious and aware of what we're doing... We’re not here to replace people.” 
:::
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-3}
![](images/camera_intelligence_vish.png){fig-alt="Headshot of Vishal Kumar, Camera Intelligence's co-founder, holding one of the company's Caira units and pointing it at the camera."}

::: figure-caption
Vishal Kumar, co-founder of Camera Intelligence.
:::

:::
:::
:::
:::

## Applying the CoSTAR Foresight Lab AI roadmap
Our AI roadmap is organised around three strategic outcomes – frameworks, targeted support, and growth – and driven by nine recommendations that seek to align technological advancement with ethical responsibility and economic opportunity, ensuring long-term growth and success of the UK screen sector.

#### How this case study aligns with the roadmap

- **Responsible AI**
: SuperScout’s decision to test and reject models that produced culturally biased outputs, choosing accuracy over convenience, is a practical example of responsible model selection. The team also has queries over data governance and domicile in the future.

- **Investment**
: SuperScout’s development has been supported by investment through the MyWorld programme, which funded the iOS app that brings the workflow into the field. The company has had a variety of funding sources and is currently exploring longer term funding.

- **Sector adaptation**
: SuperScout addresses a genuine and long-standing infrastructure gap. The reliance on Dropbox and photography portfolio sites across productions at every budget level represents exactly the friction that well-targeted technology can remove. Traction with film offices and heritage organisations points toward a potential role as shared  infrastructure for location data in the UK screen sector, created by a UK team.



## Resources

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/0CK-wsrxbng >}}

::: {.grid .gap-3 .pb-3 .pt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6}

[Find more case studies](/case-studies/index.qmd){.btn-action .btn .btn-lg .w-100 role="button"}

:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .mb-2}

[Read the report](https://a.storyblok.com/f/313404/x/ac4c0235f7/ai-in-the-screen-sector.pdf){.btn-action .btn .btn-lg .w-100 role="button"}

::: 
:::
